def: s.m. A substitute; a successor, an heir; a good son, a favourite son; a son; posterity, progeny, descendants; — adj. Filial, dutiful; — evil, depraved, corrupted
subs: s.m. A legitimate son; a dutiful son; — true or rightful successor or heir

def: s.m. (local), Mother's sister's husband ( = ḵẖālū, q.v.).
s.f. pl. (of ḵẖalq), Created things, creatures, the creation; men, people.

ety: khel + ait = Prk. आइत्तो = आयंतो = आवंतो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन् (the term. of the pres. part. with the caus. augment āpi)
def: s.m. A gambler; — a pilferer; a pickpocket.

ety: v.n. fr. خلج 'to put in motion,' &c.
def: s.m. Throbbing, beating; distraction, agitation, anxiety, uneasiness
subs: (-kā), Anxiety or uneasiness to be removed.

ety: v.n. fr. خلّ 'to become lean, or poor,' &c.; 'to pierce,' &c.
def: s.m. Break, breach, chink, gap; hiatus; interruption; rupture; disorder, derangement, unsoundness, corruptness; confusion, disturbance; ruin; — flaw, defect, imperfection; damage, injury, harm, mischief, prejudice
subs: (-meṅ), Disorder or disturbance, &c. to take place (in)
adj. & s.m. Introducing confusion, &c.; intermeddling; — one who introduces confusion, a disturber (of the peace, &c.); an intermeddler, a mar-plot
(-kā), v.n. To be an interrupter (of), to interrupt; to disturb; to interfere
s.m. Disorder of the brain, madness, craziness; melancholy
adj. Having a disordered brain, mad, crazy, confused, brain-sick, hare-brained; hot-headed

(-men), To interrupt, obstruct; to interfere, meddle; to disturb, mar, spoil, do injury (to), damage; to pervert, to turn aside (from); to impute corruptness (to); to shake, to invalidate; to prejudice (a right, &c.)
adj. & adv. Unbroken, uninterrupted; undisturbed; uninjured, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خلل (xalal), from Arabic خَلَل (ḵalal).

def: s.m. (Dakkhinī) The clattering or loud rippling of water; — adj. Wet through, drenched; weltering.
s.m. (Dakkhinī) A clattering stream; — a fountain.

ety: for A. خلالة, prob. n. of un. fr. ḵẖilāl, inf. n. iii of خلّ; see ḵẖullat
def: s.f. True or sincere friendship (in which is no ḵẖalal or defect).
s.m. Interrupter; disturber, mischief-maker, &c. ( = ḵẖalal-andāz, q.v.).
s.m. A park, chase; pleasure-ground.

ety: S. खल्ल, prob. for खण्ड+क+रः; cf. khālā
def: s.m. A hollow, cavity, pit; hollow ground, low land; — bottom.
s.f. (dialec.), A pit, &c.; a place where salt is boiled.

ety: inf. n. of خلص 'to be free from difficulty,' &c.
def: s.m. Deliverance, liberation, release, discharge, acquittal; freedom; manumission, emancipation; — exemption, immunity; — adj. Free, liberated, redeemed; done, out
subs: s.m. Deed of release; order for discharge
v.t. To liberate, release, free; to discharge, acquit; to rescue, extricate, deliver (from, -se)
v.n. To be freed, be set at liberty, be released, be discharged; to emit; — to be brought to bed, be delivered; to calve; — semen emittere; emissio seminis.

ety: = A. ḵẖilṣ, rt. خلص 'to become pure'
def: s.m. A sincere friend.
s.f. Deliverance, &c. ( = ḵẖalāṣ, q.v.); — an escape canal; — ḵẖalāṣī, or ḵẖallāṣī, s.m. A native sailor; a native artilleryman; a tent-pitcher

ety: for A. خلعة, v.n. fr. خلع 'to put off,' &c.
def: s.m.f. A dress; a robe of honour with which princes or those in authority confer dignity on subjects; a present, a gift; — approval, commendation
subs: s.m. A cess levied by the Mohammadan Government to defray the cost of robes of honour presented at court
ḵẖilʻat denā (-ko), To invest with a robe of honour.

def: s.f. Malice, wickedness; villainy; artfulness; — worthlessness; filthiness.
adj. = ḵẖālātī, q.v.; and cf. ḵẖalerā.
adj. Descended from or related to a maternal aunt (cf. ḵẖalerā)
subs: s.m. Maternal aunt's son, cousin ( = ḵẖāla-zād bhāʼī).

def: s.f. Confusion, commotion; agitation, alarm; hurry, bustle, tumult, hubbub, din, turmoil, hurly-burly
subs: (-meṅ), or khalbalī maćnā, A commotion to arise; to be in commotion, to be agitated, &c. ( = khalbalānā)
(-meṅ), or khalbalī gernā, To create a confusion, or a panic, &c.; to throw into confusion, &c.

def: s.f. Boiling, bubbling; ebullition; — commotion, confusion, &c. ( = khalbalī, q.v.).

ety: prob. onomat.; or by redupl. fr. S. क्ष्विड्
def: v.n. To boil, to bubble ( = khadbadānā); — to be tossed up and down, be agitated, be in a state of commotion; to be excited.
s.f. Confusion, commotion; agitation, alarm; hurry, bustle, tumult, hubbub, din, turmoil, hurly-burly
subs: (-meṅ), or khalbalī maćnā, A commotion to arise; to be in commotion, to be agitated, &c. ( = khalbalānā)
(-meṅ), or khalbalī gernā, To create a confusion, or a panic, &c.; to throw into confusion, &c.

def: s.m. Scented oil (cf. khalī).
s.m. A hole made by children for trap-ball, or marbles, &c.

ety: A. ḵẖāla+erā = elā = Prk. इल्लओ = S.इल+कः; cf. mauserā; phupherā, &c.
def: adj. (f. -ī), Descended from, or related to, a maternal aunt
subs: s.m. Son of a maternal aunt, cousin
s.f. Daughter of a maternal aunt, cousin.

def: s.m. = khaliś, q.v.
s.f. corr. of kalg̠ī, q.v.

ety: fr. خلو 'to be or become empty,' &c.
def: adj. Empty, vacant, void, desert; hollow, having nothing in it, blank, not filled up, not full; unoccupied, unemployed, free; exempt (from, -se); devoid, destitute (of); — without effect, ineffective, inoperative; — pure, unmingled; mere, only, sole, single, unaccompanied; — adv. Alone, by oneself, singly; — idly, unemployed; — s.m. (?), The month between the festivals of ʻīd and bakar ʻīd (so called on account of no festival occurring during that period)
subs: s.m. An egg without a tread
v.t. To empty (of, -se), to clcar or rid (of); to discharge; to vacate, to evacuate; to exhaust
(-se), To be emptied (of), to be exhausted; to be without, be devoid (of); to be free (from), &c.

def: s.f. Oil-cake (the cake that remains after the oil has been pressed out of certain seeds, especially sesamum and sarsoṅ); scented oil-cake (used as a hair-wash).
s.f. (dialec.) Chalk, &c.
subs: 'To touch the chalk'; to take (one's) first lesson.

ety: perf. part. of ḵẖalīdan; see ḵẖalish
def: part. adj. Pricked, pierced.

ety: for A. خليفة ḵẖalīfat; v.n. fr. خلف 'to succeed'
def: s.m. A successor, a sovereign (particularly applied to the successors of Mohammad), a Caliph; — (in India the term is applied to a Mohammadan cook, or tailor, or barber; and also to the son of a master or tutor, to a monitor in a school, and to a fencing master).
s.m. = khaliyān, q.v.
s.m. = khaliyān, q.v.
(fr. خلج 'to take away,' &c.), s.m. orig. 'A canal; a river,' (but in India) a gulf or bay.

ety: act. part. n. of خلق 'to measure,' &c.
def: s.m. The Creator, the Great Creator, the Originator.
ex: مخلوق میں خالق کا نظر آیا جس کو اس دیکھنے والے نے خدا کو دیکھا
maxlūq mẽ xāliq kā nazar āyā jis ko us dekhne vāle ne xudā ko dekhā
Who has seen the creator in the creation, that observer has seen God

def: (fr. خلق 'to create'), adj. lit. 'Adapted or disposed by nature'; well-disposed, of good disposition, kind, affable, benign, urbane, obliging, civil, polite.

ety: v.n. fr. خلّ 'to be poor or needy'
def: s.m. A friend, a true or sincere friend; the friend (of God), Abraham ( = ḵẖalīluʼl-lāh).
adj. Clear, pure, sheer, free from admixture; genuine, sincere; — s.m. A sincere friend.
s.m. A kind of fish, Trichopodus colisa.

ety: for A. خالصة ḵẖālisat, fem. of ḵẖālis
def: adj. Pure, &c. ( = ḵẖāliṣ); — s.m. An office of government in which the business of the revenue department is transacted, exchequer; — lands under the management of government, crown or government land; — revenue-paying lands (opp. to muʻāfī); — (in the Panjāb) a term for Sikhs who profess a pure Hinduism
subs: To be appropriated by government, to be confiscated.

ety: abst. s. fr. ḵẖal-īdan; rt. Zend har = S. sar, fr. सृ
def: s.f. (& m.?), Pricking, pain; care, solicitude, anxiety; apprehension, suspicion, misgiving; — putting a stop to, interruption
subs: (-kī), To suffer anxiety, &c.; — to reconcile; — to excite enmity.

def: s.m. An exciter of disturbance; a calumniator; a wicked person.

ety: prob. for orig. khalihān = S. खले+धान्य
def: s.m. A threshing-floor; a stack of unthreshed corn, a store of grain, corn in the straw; — a granary, a barn.
v.t. To skin, flay.
s.f. Creating; — creation; mankind; people
subs: s.f. The good of people, or of the community; the public good or welfare.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَلْق (xalq), borrowed from Arabic خَلْق (ḵalq).

def: s.m. An anklet (of gold or silver) with bells attached to it.
s.f. = khal-bal; and khaṛ-khar, qq.v.

ety: for A. خلخلة, fem. of ḵẖal-ḵẖal, fr. خل
def: adj. Worn out, thin (as a garment, or cloth); loose.
v.n. To rumble, grumble (the bowels); — v.t. To rattle; to chink (money).
s.m. Vinegar.
s.m. A shoe.
s.m. (Dakkhinī) A place where grain is trodden out by oxen, a threshing-floor (syn. khaliyān); a granary; a store of grain.
s.m. A mill; a stone or vessel for grinding drugs, &c.; — a bag.
s.m. The creator ( = ḵẖāliq).
adj. Bald-headed, bald.
s.f. See s.v. khal.

ety: khāl, q.v.+ṛā, and ṛī, Prk. अडो and अडिआ = S. र+कः and इका
def: s.f. Membrane; — prepuce, foreskin.

ety: khāl, q.v.+ṛā, and ṛī, Prk. अडो and अडिआ = S. र+कः and इका
def: s.f. Membrane; — prepuce, foreskin.
s.m. Mixture, medley, confusion, jumble
subs: s.m. = ḵẖalt̤; — adj. Mixed, mingled; confused, jumbled.

def: adv. Indeed, assuredly, certainly, truly, yes.
s.m. Maternal uncle, mother's sister's husband.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خالُو, from Arabic خَال (ḵāl).

def: adj. Bald-headed, bald ( = khalliṭ).

ety: for A. خلوة, v.n. fr. خلو 'to be empty,' &c.
def: s.f. Loneliness, solitude; seclusion, retirement, privacy; a vacant place, a private place or apartment, a closet, &c. (to which one retires for privacy); a cell (for religious retirement); — private conference
subs: s.m. Place of retirement, private apartment, closet, cell, oratory; the women's apartment
s.f. Privacy; behaviour or conduct in private
To retire; to be closeted (with, -se), to confer in private (with)
s.f. A solitary place; a place of retirement and seclusion; retirement
and ḵẖalwat-nishīn, adj. & s.m. lit. 'Choosing solitude' and 'sitting in solitude' retired, recluse; — a solitaire; a hermit

def: adj. & s.m. Of or appertaining to solitude; solitary; — a solitaire; a hermit; — one admitted into the private apartments; a confidant, an intimate friend
s.f. Mother's sister, maternal aunt

adj. Raw, unripe, green, crude, immature; inexpert, inexperienced; vain, puerile, absurd; not solid or substantial, not made of masonry, of unbaked earth or of mud; rough, approximate (as an estimate, &c.); imperfect, unsound, bad; lower, smaller (weight or measure, — opp. to puḵẖta, 'the larger' of two standard weights)
subs: s.f. Gross receipts or collections (of revenue)
A term of abuse applied to a woman who has been cohabited with before the age of maturity; — a sharp or cunning woman; a prude
s.m. A rough statement or account
adj. & s.m. Inexpert, awkward; — a bungler
s.f. The entertaining of vain desires; foolish desire
s.m. An estate which is under direct management (and not leased)
s.f. Inexpertness, unskilfulness, awkwardness; — a writing reed, a pen (see ḵẖāma)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خام (xām) from Proto-Iranian *HaHmáh, ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *h₂eh₃mós (“raw, uncooked”). Doublet of آم (ām).

def: adj. & s.m. Curved, twisted, bent, crooked, bowed; curled; coiled; — a bend, curve, crook; a curl, knot, ringlet; a coil, fold, ply; crookedness, curvature; bending, flexure; — the part of a noose which encircles the neck; a noose; — the upper arm
subs: adj. Twist within twist; curly; — s.m. Curling locks; — ḵẖam bajānā, ḵẖam ṭhoṅknā (apne), To strike the hand against the arms (preparatory to wrestling), to challenge (as wrestlers do)
s.m. Graceful motion; — coquetry, blandishment (of a mistress); — adj. Elegant, graceful
s.m. The curved stick with which hockey-on-horseback is played
adj. Curved, twisted, bent, crooked, &c. ( = ḵẖam)

def: s.m. corr. of خم 'upper arm,' q.v. (for phrases).
s.m. (dialec.) Post, pillar, &c.; — mast (of a ship).
s.m. contrac. of khambh, q.v.
s.m. A writing-reed, a pen
s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode.

s.m. A species of cowhage, Mucuna nivea (the tender fleshy pods of which, when stripped of their exterior skin, make an excellent vegetable for the table, scarcely inferior, to the garden-bean of Europe).
s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode.
v.t. To bend, twist, crook, bow; to coil; to curl.

ety: Prk. खम्म = S. घर्म; s = sā, 'like,' q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Close, sultry, hot; — the hot or sultry season.
v.t. (Dakh.) = khapasnā, q.v.

ety: Prk. खंभो and खंभओ = S. स्तम्भः and स्तम्भ+कः
def: s.f. A pillar, a column; a stake, post, pole
subs: m. plur. The two perpendicular beams at the mouth of a well, supporting a third transverse beam to which the bucket-pulley is fixed.

ety: Prk. खंभो and खंभओ = S. स्तम्भः and स्तम्भ+कः
def: s.f. A pillar, a column; a stake, post, pole
subs: m. plur. The two perpendicular beams at the mouth of a well, supporting a third transverse beam to which the bucket-pulley is fixed.

def: s.m. See کهمبهہ khambh, and کهمبها khambhā.

ety: Prk. खंभो and खंभओ = S. स्तम्भः and स्तम्भ+कः
def: s.f. A pillar, a column; a stake, post, pole
subs: m. plur. The two perpendicular beams at the mouth of a well, supporting a third transverse beam to which the bucket-pulley is fixed.

def: s.m. See کهمبهہ khambh, and کهمبها khambhā.

ety: Prk. खंभो and खंभओ = S. स्तम्भः and स्तम्भ+कः
def: s.f. A pillar, a column; a stake, post, pole
subs: m. plur. The two perpendicular beams at the mouth of a well, supporting a third transverse beam to which the bucket-pulley is fixed.

def: s.m. See کهمبهہ khambh, and کهمبها khambhā.
s.f. Rawness, unripeness, immaturity; inexperience; imperfection, defect, fault; weakness, infirmity (of a title, &c.); — loss.
s.f. Crookedness; bending, bend, curvature, flexure.

ety: perf. part. of ḵẖamīdan; see ḵẖam
def: part. adj. Curved, bent, crooked, awry ( = ḵẖam)
subs: adj. Bent in form or stature.

def: s.f. Crookedness; curvature.
adj. To be bent, fit to be bent.
s.m. Sleepiness, drowsiness, heaviness; lassitude, fatigue, weariness.
s.m. Leaven, ferment; kneaded and leavened dough; — composition, constitution; earth, clay
s.m. Leaven, &c. ( = ḵẖamīr); — conserve (of roses or violets); a thick syrup; — a kind of tobacco.
adj. Leavened; — a sort of leavened bread.
adj. Much given to drink; crop-sick.
v.t. To close (the mouth of a vessel) with earth, or flour, &c.; to close, to seal, fasten (a letter, &c.)
adj. Silent; — s.m. A disease in horses.
adj. Silent; dumb; — intj. Be silent! silence!
s.m. pl. (of ḵẖamosh), The silent ones, the dead.
s.f. Silence, taciturnity.
s.f. Silence.

ety: inf. n. of خمر 'to veil or cover'
def: s.m. Covering, concealing; clouding; — fermenting, leavening; wine, spirituous or fermented liquor.
adj. fem. of ḵẖamsa, 'five,' q.v. (used only in Arabic phrases).

ety: for A. خمسة ḵẖamsat; rt. خمس 'to take the fifth,' &c.
def: adj. Five; — s.m. An aggregate of five poetical pieces; a stanza of five lines.

ety: khamas, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Closeness, sultriness, heat ( = ghamas).
adj. = P خامشي ḵẖāmushī, s.f. = ḵẖāmosh, and ḵẖāmoshī, qq.v.
s.f. = P خامش ḵẖāmush, adj. = ḵẖāmosh, and ḵẖāmoshī, qq.v.

ety: kham, q.v.+yāza, rt. Zend yāś = S. यश्
def: s.m. Stretching; yawning, gaping; — stretching by way of punishment, putting on the rack; punishment, retribution, reward, fruit
subs: ḵẖamyāza bharnā, ḵẖamyāza bhugatnā = ḵẖamyāza khaiṅćnā (2), q.v.
1˚ To stretch; to yawn; — 2˚ To suffer the punishment (of), to reap the fruit (of; — e.g. ham-ne is-kām-kā ḵẖūb ḵẖamyāza khaiṅćā).

ety: Pehl. ahu; Zend aṅhu; S. asu; cf. also S. svāmin
def: s.m. 'Lord of lords,' a prime minister.
s.f. A mine; a quarry; — (met.) receptacle; nest; heap, abundance.
s.m. Division (of a house), storey, flight (of rooms).
s.m. A mine.
s.f. Clink, tinkle, jingle, &c.
s.m. The sky, the air (see kha); — the hold of a ship.
s.m. A moment, an instant (cf. ćhin or ćhan); — a certain day of the fortnight, as the full of the moon, the change of the moon, &c.

ety: Prk. खाअणअं; S. खादनीयं, rt. खाद्
def: v.t. To eat, feed on; to consume; to swallow; to drink; to inhale; to take (medicine, or the air, or a bribe, or an oath); to embezzle; (met.) to get, receive; to feel, suffer, endure; to hold, contain; to harass, plague, pester (usually with jān, e.g. jān khānā); — s.m. Eating; food, meal, dinner
subs: (intens. of khānā), To eat up, swallow up, devour, consume; to embezzle, to make away with; to leave out, omit; to eat away, rub or wear away, corrode, &c.
v.t. To eat up, &c. (intens. of khānā, q.v.)
s.m. Meat and drink, food, victuals, fare, board; — khāne-jog, adj. Fit to eat, eatable, edible
or khāne-hārā, or khān-hārā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Eating; corrosive; caustic, escharotic; — eater, feeder, consumer, devourer; a mouth to feed.

def: s.m. = khaṇḍ, q.v. (and cf. khan, 'a storey').

ety: prob. rel. n. fr. ḵẖān, q.v.
def: s.m. House, dwelling, place; receptacle, socket, drawer, partition, compartment; pigeon-hole (of a desk); square (of a chess-board, &c.); column (of a tabular statement, &c.); head (in an account or statement); division, class; department
subs: 'May your house flourish!' (used by way of benediction); — adj. Flourishing, prosperous
s.m. An inclosed garden, a garden adjoining a house; a kitchen garden

adj. & s.m. 'Having his house on his back'; wandering from place to place, having no settled habitation, nomadic; — one who has no fixed residence, a rover, wanderer, a traveller; a pilgrim; a gipsy; a vagrant; — a nomadic tribe
s.f. Destruction of a house or family, breaking up of a home (chiefly by the death of the mistress)
s.f. Filling in the columns (of), entry (of, -kī)
s.f. The act of searching a house, search of a house
s.m. Flowers worked on net or muslin; the work itself
To exceed the amount at credit
s.m. A breeder of domestic quarrels; a breeder of disturbances, a brawler; a duellist
s.f. Domestic or family quarrels; intestine broils, civil war; bickering; skirmish, duel, disturbance, affray
adj. & s.m. Ruined, destroyed; base, abject; — a vain, empty fellow, a good-for-nothing fellow, a vagabond, a wretch
s.f. The state of the master of a family; housekeeping
s.m. A son-in-law who lives in the house of his wife's father (by whom he is adopted as a son)
adj. & s.m. Fond of staying at home (a term of abuse)
s.m. 'Born in the house'; child of a slave, a slave
adj. & s.m. House-burning, destructive (fire); — a house-burner, an incendiary
adj. & s.m. Sitting in the house, unemployed, idle; retired; — one out of employ; a retired officer; a pensioner.

def: adj. Relating to a house, domestic, household; private, personal; — domesticated, tame; — s.f. A kept woman; a clandestine prostitute
subs: s.m. A domestic affair; a private business or matter.

def: s.m. pl. (of ḵẖāna, q.v.), Houses, dwellings, &c.
s.m. Strangulation, suffocation; the quinsy.
s.m. (f. -ikā), One who digs, a digger; a miner.
s.f. Ring, clink, jingle, chink; — a high wind.

r: ḵẖānaqāh, ḵẖānaqah, ḵẖāna, gāh, gah, Sūfī
def: = P خانقه ḵẖānaqah (P. ḵẖāna+gāh or gah, arabicised), s.f. orig. 'A convent for Sūfī recluses; a convent, monastery, a religious establishment for holy men.

r: ḵẖānaqah, ḵẖānaqāh, ḵẖāna, gāh, gah, Sūfī
def: = A خانقاه ḵẖānaqāh (P. ḵẖāna+gāh or gah, arabicised), s.f. orig. 'A convent for Sūfī recluses; a convent, monastery, a religious establishment for holy men.
v.n. & t. = khanaknā, q.v.
v.n. & t. To clink, ring, jingle, clank; to chink (money).
s.f. A lady, a woman of rank, a princess; title of the wife of a ḵẖān.
s.m. pl. (of ḵẖinzīr), Hogs; — glandular swellings, or ulcers, in the neck; scrofula.

ety: prob. Prk. कच्छो and कच्छओ; S. कच्छः and कच्छ+ कः
def: s.m. Mud, mire, slush, slime; muddy or marshy soil, quagmire.

ety: prob. S. कर्ष+कः; cf. khoṅć, khoṅs, khaiṅć
def: s.m. A pit, hole, cavity, hollow; a notch, incision; a rent, rip; a gap, chasm; a break, hitch; delay; a turn or bend (in a road), a warp (in a wall); a loss (in trade); discrepancy (in an account, &c.); — a basket (made of twigs, or split bamboo), a pannier; a hen-coop or cage.

ety: prob. Prk. कच्छो and कच्छओ; S. कच्छः and कच्छ+ कः
def: s.m. Mud, mire, slush, slime; muddy or marshy soil, quagmire.
s.f. A basket, &c. ( = khaṅćiyā).

ety: dim. of khāṅćā, and = khāṅćī, q.v.
def: s.f. A small basket; a small hen-coop, &c.

s.m. Piece, fragment, part, portion; division (of a house, or a country, or of the earth); subdivision, section (of a work), chapter; apartment (of a house, &c.); side, quarter; district, province, region (much used in the comp. of Indian names of places, e.g. Bundel-khaṇḍ, Anglice, 'Bundlecund'); — coarse sugar ( = khāṅṛ); — a flaw (in a jewel); — the songs of Padmāvat; — a kind of song composed and sung by washermen
subs: s.f. Section of a country, a state, a province, county, district
To compose a khaṇḍ or song; to recite a khaṇḍ.

def: s.m. corr. of khaṇḍ, q.v. (used in comp., e.g. Bundel-khand).
s.m. A sugar-boiler, sugar-manufacturer
subs: To boil down sugar to a hard consistence, to crystallize sugar

ety: fr. ḵẖandīdan; rt. Zend qar; S. sur
def: adj. & s.m. Laughing, smiling; a laugh; laughter; — a laughing-stock
subs: adj. Having a smiling or open brow, eheerful
adj. Of a smiling countenance, cheerful
ḵẖanda-zanān, adj. & s.m. Setting up a laugh; — one who sets up a laugh.

def: s.m. Anything broken; broken rice; — a broken board or tablet.
s.m. contrac. of khāṇḍā, q.v.

ety: prob. S. खण्ड+कः; but cf. खड्गः
def: s.m. A straight double-edged sword; a cutlass; a (butcher's) cleaver; — a piece; a flake or slice (of fish, &c.)
subs: 'To walk on the edge of a sword'; to move very steadily and cautiously (not swaying to right or left); to administer strictly impartial justice.

def: (P. kanda arabicized), s.f.(m.?), Ditch, fosse, moat.
s.m. (Dakkhinī) A level plain (syn. maidān).
adj. & s.m. = khaṅḍar, q.v.
s.m. A piece (as of land, &c.); a flake, slice (of fruit, &c.).

s.m. Reducing (anything) to pieces, cutting to pieces; breaking; dividing; cutting, intersecting; destroying, demolishing, annihilating; interrupting, preventing, averting, removing; thwarting; revoking; rescinding; refuting, controverting
subs: v.t. To break to pieces, cut to pieces, to divide; to destroy, demolish, &c. &c.

def: s.m. Family, household; race, lineage, descent, house (of a prince, &c.), dynasty
subs: Royal family; house, dynasty; — ḵẖāndān-kī 'alāmat, s.f. Armorial bearings.

def: adj. Smiling, laughing (at, -se); merry.

ety: prob. khātā or khattā, q.v.+aff. nā; or fr. S. खण्ड
def: s.m. A ditch; a hole, pit; a pit from which earth has been dug to make bricks or pottery, a clay-pit ( = khadānā); — an incision, a notch (in a piece of wood).
v.t. (rustic), To cause to run off, to chase away; — to pack off, despatch, send (on an errand, &c.).
adj. Relating to the family, &c.; descended from father to son, hereditary, ancestral, family; of good family, of rank.
s.m. = P خانداري ḵẖān-dārī, s.f. = ḵẖāna-dār, and ḵẖāna-dārī, q.v.s.v. ḵẖāna.

ety: prob. S. खण्ड+आलः or रः; Prk. खंडालो, or खंडरो (?)
def: adj. & s.m. Destroyed, ruined, in ruins, dilapidated; — a ruin, a dilapidated building or tenement; a mass of ruins (as of a village or town); — a break, gap, hole, cavity, chasm, abyss; broken or raviny ground.
s.f. = P خاندار ḵẖān-dār, s.m. = ḵẖāna-dār, and ḵẖāna-dārī, q.v.s.v. ḵẖāna.
s.m. corr. of khanjarīṭ, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = khaṅḍar, q.v.
subs: s.m. Name of a sect of Jains.

def: adj. & s.m. = khaṅḍar, q.v.
subs: s.m. Name of a sect of Jains.

def: s.m. = khanḍ, q.v.
s.m. corr. of ḵẖandaq, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = khaṅḍar, q.v.

ety: Prk. खंड+उल्लो; S. खण्ड+उलः or ऊलः
def: adj. Disturbed; turbid.
s.f. A woman given to laughing and joking with men; — a wanton, shameless woman (a term of abuse); — a coquette.

part. (f. -ā), Cut, torn, broken in pieces; scattered, dispersed, destroyed; broken (as faith, or allegiance, &c.); interrupted; averted, prevented; revoked; refuted, controverted; disappointed; betrayed
subs: v.t. To break, destroy; to interrupt; to prevent; to refute, &c.

def: s.f. A woman whose husband or lover has been guilty of infidelity; a woman who is incensed at the infidelity of her husband or lover, and makes free use of abuse, but retains no malice in her breast; a suspicious, jealous wife.

ety: khāṅḍ˚ = Prk. खंड(इ), or खंडे(इ) = S. खण्डय(ति), rt. खण्ड्
def: v.t. To break to pieces, to pound, to crush; to excavate.

ety: khand˚ = Prk. खंड(इ) = S. खण्डय(ति), rt. खण्ड्
def: v.t. To break or cut to pieces, &c. ( = khaṇḍan karnā).

ety: khand = S. खण्ड+dim. aff. olī, fr. S. र or ल+इका, with conjunct vowel o
def: s.f. = khindṛī, q.v.
s.m. = khāg, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Tusked; having large projecting teeth.
s.m. = khaṅgar, q.v.; — 2˚ adj. = khaṅgail, q.v.
v.t. To wash, to wash out, to rinse
s.m. (rustic) = khaṅkhār, q.v.
s.m. Name of a low and despised race or tribe.
s.m. Burnt or vitrified brick; any hard or dry substance; — refuse or dross (of iron, &c.), scoria (of metal)
subs: (-ko), To harden and dry up, to be reduced to a skeleton.

def: s.m. Burnt or vitrified brick; any hard or dry substance; — refuse or dross (of iron, &c.), scoria (of metal)
subs: (-ko), To harden and dry up, to be reduced to a skeleton.

def: v.t. = next, q.v.; — v.n. = khaṅkhārnā.
v.t. = khaṅgālnā, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ī), See s.v. khānā.
s.f. A pit or hole in which rice in the husks (or other grain) is kept (syn. khattā, q.v.).
s.f. A mine ( = khān, q.v.).
s.f. = khāʼī, q.v.
adj. Of or relating to a mine; — s.m. Opening or hole (in a wall), breach.
s.f. = khāʼī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Limping, lame, crippled; — a lame man, a cripple.
s.f. A species of metre in which there is a stanza of two lines, the one containing thirty-two feet and the other thirty.
s.m. The wagtail, Motacilla alba, or Enicurus maculatus; — the spotted fork-tail.
s.m. A large knife, a dagger (generally curved and double-edged), a poniard, a hanger.
ex: قاتل جِس کی زَد سے خُود محفُوظ رہ سکے ایسا کوئی خنجر نہیں دیکھا بہُت دنوں سے
an assassin who can remain safe from his own blows – I've not seen such a dagger in some time now
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَنْجَر (xanjar). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi خنجر (xnjr /⁠xañjar⁠/).

def: s.m. corr. of خنجر ḵẖanjar, q.v.
s.f. corr. of خنجري ḵẖanjarī, q.v.
s.f. A mode of printing or staining silk (gul-badan); striped silk; stripes.
s.f. A small tambourine.
s.m. The wagtail, Motacilla alba, or Enicurus maculatus; — the spotted fork-tail.
adj. & s.m. (dialec.) = khaṅkh, q.v.
s.f. The plant polypody, a kind of moss.
v.t. = khanaknā, q.v.
s.m. = khaṅkhār, q.v.
v.n. = khaṅkhārnā, q.v.

adj. & s.m. (dialec.; cf. khaṅkhaṛ), Old, decrepit, worn out; dry, withered; weak, feeble; poor, penniless, wretched, miserable; mean, sordid, miserly; — savage, morose, crabbed, cross-grained; — fiery; vicious (a horse); — a decrepit old man; a poor wretch; — poor soil, land that requires to be left fallow for a year or two.
s.f. A decrepit, or a wrinkled old woman; a hag; a jade.
s.m. corr. of kan-khajūrā, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. khan, 'clink'; & cf. khankhanānā.

ety: khankhan˚, by redupl. of S. क्कण; — cf. S. खणखणाय
def: v.n. & t. To ring, clink, clank, tinkle, jingle; to chink (money).

ety: fr. A. ḵẖan-ḵẖana, rt. ḵẖan, 'to make a sound from the nose'
def: v.n. To snuffle, to speak through the nose.

ety: khaṅkh, q.v.+āʼo = Ap. Prk. अअउ; Prk. अ अं = अत्तं = S. क+त्वं
def: s.m. A blemish, or a vice (in a horse).

ety: S. कक्खटः, or खक्खटः, prob. Prk. forms
def: adj. & s.m. Hard, dry, shrivelled, shrunken up, wasted and worn (with age), decayed; — anything very dry; a shrivelled or dried tree or plant, &c.
s.m. The sound of clearing the throat; the rattling of phlegm in the throat; phlegm; expectoration.
adj. & s.m. Hard, dry and crackling, dried up, shrivelled, withered (cf. khaṅkh); — anything hard, or dry and crackling; — a skeleton.
s.m. Pit, shaft (of a mine).
v.n. To clear the throat of phlegm, to hem, hawk; to expectorate; to cough.
s.m. House and home, household furniture, everything belonging to the house; household, family
subs: adj. Having a ruined or desolated home; ruined, desolated.

def: s.m. Conceit, vanity
subs: v.n. To walk or act conceitedly, to be affected, to strut, &c.; to swagger; to be quarrelsome.

def: v.t. To dig; to scrape.

ety: v.n. fr. خنس 'to retire; to shrink'
def: s.m. The devil (so called because he retires or shrinks at the mention of God); — (met.) a wicked person.
adj. Devilish, Satanic; — s.f. Devilishness.
s.m. = ḵẖānmān, q.v.
s.f. = khāp, q.v.

ety: khān = khānā; or fr. S. खादनं
def: s.m. Eating and drinking, feeding; food and drink; allowance of food, &c., maintenance.
s.f.m.(?) = khāṅḍ, q.v.
s.m. = khāṅḍā, q.v.
s.f. = khaṛāṅʼo, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = khaṅḍar.
s.m. A taster of provisions (to a king).

r: ḵẖānsāmān, ḵẖānsāmāṅ, ḵẖān, sāmā n, lit
def: (ḵẖān+sāmā n) s.m. lit. 'Master of the stores,' a house steward, butler, major-domo.
s.f. The office or department which has charge of all expenses in a great man's house.
s.f. A cough

ety: khāṅs˚ = Prk. खास(इ), or कास(इ) = S. कास(ते), rt. कास्
def: v.n. To cough; — s.m. Coughing.
v.n. 1˚ = khasnā, q.v.; — 2˚ = khāṅsnā, q.v.
s.f. A paddle for digging the ground with, a dibble.
s.m. A trench, a ditch (round a field), &c., see khāt.
s.m. Family, house, &c. ( = ḵẖāndān); a race or tribe of people; a great tribe, an illustrious family; — the mistress of a family.

ety: v.n. fr. khāpnā = khapānā, caus. of khapnā, q.v.
def: s.f. A sheath, scabbard, case; — stalk or stem (of the plantain); — a slice (of fruit, &c.); — a clan, sect.
s.f. A piece torn off (from a bamboo, &c.), a splinter, a piece of split wood; (met.) a very lean person, a 'lath.'
s.f. A splinter, &c. ( = khapāć, q.v.); — a clinging to, clutch, grasp, grip, close embrace, hug
subs: (-kī), To grasp or clutch, to hold in close embrace, to hug.

ety: caus. of khapnā; — khapā = Prk. खपाव˚ or खपावे˚ = S. क्षप+आपय˚, caus. of rt. क्षि
def: v.t. To destroy, make an end of, make away with, to despatch; to ruin, ravage, lay waste; to end, finish, complete, exhaust; to use, expend, consume, waste; to take in or into, to swallow up; to absorb: to dry up; to mix; to cause to enter, to put or fit (into).
s.f. = khapāć, q.v.
s.m. Consumption, expenditure, use, employment, &c.; — requirement, request, demand, &c. ( = khapat, q.v.).
s.f. A heavier sort of plough for stiff soil.

ety: prob. fr. S. क्षाप˚, caus. of rt. क्षि
def: adj. & s.m. (rustic) Old, worn out, decrepit; — old and experienced; — a decrepit old man.

ety: S. क्षपत, fr. क्षप्, fr. caus. of क्षि
def: part. Destroyed, lost; made away with; sold; wasted, consumed, expended; deranged, demented.

ety: khap(nā) + Prk. अत्ती, and अत्तिआ = S. आप्ति and आप्तिका (i.e. āpi, caus. aug.+ti, and ti+kā)
def: s.f. Consumption, expenditure, expense; requisition, request, demand, vent, sale, market; — place, room, space (e.g. yahāṅ tumhārī khapat nahīṅ hone-kī).

ety: khapā, stem of khapānā, q.v.+ṭā = S. त्र+कं
def: s.m. (lit. 'What sticks to, or tears,' &c.), A large branch broken or torn off a tree; a large piece of split wood (cf. khapāć).
s.f. dim. of khapāṭā, q.v.
s.f. A small piece, or slice (of fruit, &c.).
s.m. (f. -ī), A dialec. form of khapṭā or khapṛā, qq.v.

ety: khap˚ = Prk. खप्प(इ) = S. क्षप्य(ते), fr. क्षप्, caus. of क्षि
def: v.n. To be destroyed, be wiped out, be disposed of, be killed or slain; to be made away with, to be swallowed up; to be consumed; to be expended, to be used, to be exhausted; to be lost (in, -meṅ); to be absorbed (by, -meṅ), to be dried up; — to obtain a vent or market, to find sale, to go off, to sell; — to be fitted (into); to fit or go (into); to enter, penetrate (into, -meṅ); to be mixed up (with, -meṅ); to mix (with), join or unite (with); to remain; — s.m. A company (of travellers, &c.); a crowd

s.m. Skull, cranium; — a gourd-shell, or an earthen cup (used by Jogīs or mendicants), a beggar's bowl or dish; a chafing-dish (syn. ṭhīk, ṭhīkrā); — a vessel in which the blood of a victim is offered
subs: (-kā), 'To fill a Jogī's or mendicant's dish,' to give alms (to a Jogī, in the name of Śiva); to make a blood-offering.

def: s.m. = khapṭā, q.v.
s.m. A tile (for roofing); — a kind of weapon, a broad-headed arrow.
s.m. (f. -ī) = khapṭā, q.v.
s.m. A tiled house or building; tiling; a tile
subs: A window built of semicircular tiles

def: s.f. (dialec. or rustic), 1˚ = khaprī; — 2˚ = khoprī, qq.v.
s.f. A small tile; — the skull ( = khappar).

ety: khap, fr. khapnā+dim. aff. ṭā = ṛā = S. र+क
def: s.m. (f. -ī), A detached piece (of wood, bark, skin, &c.), a slip or splinter (of wood); a plank; a lath; a scale, scab; a peeling; a broken tile, potsherd; a small piece, a slice (of fruit, &c.); — a grub which destroys wheat, &c. (cf. khaprā, khapṛā, khaplā).
adj. & s.m. Determined, tenacious, dogged; — deranged, demented, insane, mad; — a determined or dogged person; — a madman, lunatic.

ety: khap(nā) + Prk. अत्ती, and अत्तिआ = S. आप्ति and आप्तिका (i.e. āpi, caus. aug.+ti, and ti+kā)
def: s.f. Consumption, expenditure, expense; requisition, request, demand, vent, sale, market; — place, room, space (e.g. yahāṅ tumhārī khapat nahīṅ hone-kī).
s.m. The betel-nut tree, Areca faufel or catechu.

ety: rt. Zend kar = S. kar, fr. kr̤ī
def: s.m. A thorn, prickle, spine; thistle, bramble; a spur, a cock's spur; — bristle, hair
subs: Anything disagreeable to the sight, an eye-sore
s.f. A fence, or temporary defence, made of thorns
adj. Thorny, prickly, bristling; barbed; troublesome, arduous; — having a beard, bearded
s.f. = ḵẖār-istān, s.m. A place of thorns, thistles, &c.; a thicket, a brake
(-par), To feel or have a thorn in the flesh; to feel jealous or envious (of)
v.n. To be a thorn in the side, be an eye-sore, be disagreeable (to).
wety: From Classical Persian خار (xār, “thorn”), from Middle Persian hʾl (xār, “thorn”).

def: s.m. Alkali; potash; impure carbonate of potash or of soda; — an escharotic, the potential country caustic; — saltness, brackishness; barrenness
subs: v.n. To be impregnated with alkaline salts.

def: s.f. A creek or bay, &c. ( = khāl, q.v.).
s.m. Rough or rugged ground; ground cut up by rain, &c. (syn. behar).
s.f. Grass, straw; coarse long grass (for thatching); a weed; — loppings; sweepings, rubbish.

adj. Hard, harsh; rough, rugged, notched, jagged; — sharp, keen, cutting, severe; pointed; pungent, biting; hot; solid, dense; brittle; hoarse; — s.m. (f. -ī), An ass (cf. P. ḵẖar); — heat (of the sun, &c.; syn. ghām); — name of a Daitya or demon; — a Daitya or demon (in general)
subs: (˚ra+ari), s.m. 'The enemy of the demon Khar, or of Khar and Dūshan,' an epithet of Rām (who slew those demons)
s.m. (in Gram.) A table of vowels
s.m. The demon brothers Khar and Dūshaṇ.

def: s.f. Grass, straw; grass and trees.
s.m. An ass
subs: adj. Doubly or emphatically an ass, very stupid
s.m. The wild ass, the onager
adj. Stupid, pig-headed, perverse, obstinate; cross-grained, ill-tempered; — proud; boastful
adj. Lusty; athletic; — obstinate; stupid; — lustful, lascivious, lewd
ḵẖar-nafsa, adj. Magnum penem habens; — lustful, &c. ( = ḵẖar-mast)

def: s.m. A hard stone, a flint; — a kind of undulated silk-cloth; — adj. Hard, flinty
subs: s.m. Granite; a kind of marble
adj. Stone-splitting; strong and sharp (as rats' teeth, &c.).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Alkaline; salt, saline, brackish; bitter
subs: s.f. Saline soil; soil impregnated with alkali
or khārī namak, s.f. Alkaline salt; a kind of sulphate of soda (used in medicine, and for the adulteration of common salt).

def: s.m. A net (for holding grass or straw, &c.).

adj. (f. -ī), Good, excellent, best, prime, choice; genuine, real, true, not false or counterfeit; pure, undebased, unalloyed; — true, faithful, honest, upright, ingenuous, sincere, candid; just, fair, right, exact, strict; complete, full, standard (as weight, &c.); — fixed, settled; valid (as a bargain, &c.)
subs: v.t. To separate good (money, &c.) from bad
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Good and bad; — the good and the bad
A good paymaster, one who pays cash; a trustworthy person
(vulg. khaṛī bolī), Pure language or idiom; coin of the true mint

ety: prob. Prk. खड्ढओ; S. स्कब्ध+कः, rt. स्कम्भ्
def: adj. (f. -ī), Standing up, erect, upright, on-end; perpendicular; very steep or high; — standing, stationary; — at anchor, moored; aground; — in waiting, at call, remaining; — continual, perpetual, constant; — ready, prepared; ripe; — half-cooked, undissolved (e.g. khaṛī dāl); — in abeyance, standing over, not determined upon, of which payment is suspended (a bill or draft); unclosed, not finally and duly entered (an account, &c.); — s.m. A perpendicular
subs: 'Not to stand and drink water' (at a person's house); to hate the very sight of (a person), to hate cordially
To be struck down suddenly or unexpectedly
'To beat (the head, or breast) standing and lying down,' to lament without intermission
v.t. To keep standing, or erect, &c.; to support, sustain
v.n. To remain standing, to stand, stay, wait
Upright figure; stature of a man standing upright

or kar-denā, v.t. To cause to stand up or rise, to lift up; to raise, erect, set up, stick up; to pitch (a tent); to erect, construct, build; to set up, start, open, establish, institute; to begin, commence; — to make stationary, bring to a stand, to stop; — to fix, place, station; — to invent, fabricate; to procure a fictitious person for some purpose; — to raise, rouse, stir up, excite; — to sew with long stitches, to baste; — khaṛa khet, A standing crop; (in Geom.) a vertical plane

or ho-jānā, v.n. To be or become erect, to stand up, to rise; to stand; to rear (a horse); — to be raised, be lifted; to be built, be constructed; to be set on foot, be started, be opened, be established, be instituted; to begin; to arise; — to be ready, be prepared
s.m. An account drawn out on a loose sheet of paper; — a day-book, journal
s.m. A slab of stone placed under a pillar or post; plinth (of a column); a flat stone having a hole bored in it in which a pole is set upright
v.t. To balance (an account), to strike a balance
or khaṛe-pāʼoṅ, adv. On foot, standing all the while; — on the spot, at the instant, quickly, promptly, expeditiously, soon; instantly, immediately; — for a short while
s.m. Washing and bleaching roughly or quickly at the ghāṭ or washing place (in the river, &c.); (fig.) the first rough doing (of a thing); — cleanness (as of a just-bleached cloth)
A play in which various professions (as grocers, cobblers, &c.) are represented (it is so called from the lively music which accompanies the representation)
A hunḍī or bill as yet unaccepted.

ety: kharā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Goodness, excellence; genuineness, reality, trueness; truth; faithfulness, trustiness, trustworthiness, probity, rectitude, integrity, honesty, sincerity, candour; pureness; justness, rightness, fitness, exactness.
adj. a dialec. form of khaṛā, q.v.

r: khaṛā, kharā, khaṛī bolī, kharī bolī
def: adj. prop. kharā, q.v. (khaṛī bolī = kharī bolī).

ety: inf. n. of خرب 'to be or become in a state of ruin'; — used as an epithet
def: adj. Ruined, spoiled, depopulated, wasted, deserted, desolate; abandoned, lost, miserable, wretched; bad, worthless, vitiated, corrupt, reprobate, noxious, vicious, depraved, profligate; defiled, polluted, contaminated
subs: ḵẖarāb-ḥāl, or ḵẖarāb-ḵẖasta, adj. Ruined, desolated; broken down (in circumstances), wretched, distressed
v.t. To ruin, mar, spoil; to waste, depredate, depopulate, lay waste, desolate; to vitiate, corrupt, deprave; to pollute, defile; to debauch, to violate
adj. Wandering about homeless and destitute; wandering about to no purpose.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خراب (xarāb), from Arabic خَرَاب (ḵarāb).

ety: for A. خرابة ḵẖarābat, fem. of ḵẖarāb
def: adj. Ruined, &c. ( = ḵẖarāb, q.v.); — s.m. Ruin, devastation, desolation; a waste, waste land.
s.f. A tavern; — a brothel (such being usually kept in ruins).
s.m. A haunter of taverns and brothels, a debauchee, a rake.
s.f. Ruin, destruction, desolation; badness, corruption, depravity; noxiousness, ill, evil, mischief, perdition; misery, trouble, affliction; difficulty, perplexity
v.t. To turn on a lathe; (met.) to worry, to torment.
s.f. = H کهرگهہ खर्घ khargh, s.m. dialec. forms of khaṛag, q.v.
s.f. Smell of burnt grass or straw; offensive smell; — a burnt taste; a saltish taste.
s.m. A currycomb

ety: khaṛā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता + इका
def: s.f. Erectness, uprightness, straightness, steepness, &c.
adj. & s.m. = kharkharā, and kharahrā, qq.v.
s.m. Tax, tribute, impost, duty, revenue (syn. kar)
subs: s.m. Land tax; revenue from land
adj. & s.m. Paying tax, or tribute, or rent; — a tributary; one who pays revenue to Government
(-par), To tax, to assess; to levy tribute or a contribution (on).

def: adj. Subject to taxation, revenue-paying (land); tributary.
s.m. prop. khaṛak, q.v.
s.f. Sound of knocking, rapping, &c. (see khaṭak).
s.m. Cow-house, cow-shed; sheep-pen; — pound (for cattle).
s.m. A crash, clang, rattle, loud report ( = khaṛākā).
s.m. = khaṭākā, or kharākā, q.v.

v.n. To knock, rap, bang, rattle, ring, clatter, crash; to rustle; to stir, shake; — to wrangle, quarrel; — to be startled, to start; to be frightened away, be scared; — to be warned, be apprized (by rattling, or other noise)
subs: v.n. (intens.), To be scared; — to be warned, be apprized (of).

def: v.t. To grind or rub down.
s.f.(m.?) = kharal, q.v.
s.f. = kharāhand, q.v.
s.m. = khaṛanjā, q.v.
s.m. A row or layer (of bricks, in a building, &c.) laid upright or on edge; a pavement made of bricks or stone laid on edge, a causeway.
adj. Dry, dried up, shrivelled, parched; — acid.
= H کهرنکهہ खरंख kharaṅkh, adj. dialec. forms of khaṛaṅk, &c., q.v.
